# AI and LLM Agent Instructions for binarystruct-codegen

This file gives an LLM agent the selection surface, invocation contract, expected
behavior, and known traps needed to drive binarystruct-codegen correctly on the
first try.

> **Source of truth:** the flag definitions in `main.go` and the generation
> behavior in `generator.go`, plus the parent library's ground-truth
> [SPECIFICATION.md](../SPECIFICATION.md). This manual is *derived* — if they
> disagree, the code wins, and `binarystruct-codegen -h` is authoritative for flags.

## 1. Selection surface — use when / not for

- **Use when:** you have Go structs annotated with binarystruct `binary:"..."` tags
  and want **static, (near-)reflection-free** marshal/unmarshal methods generated at
  build time — typically wired with a `//go:generate` directive for production hot
  paths. The generated methods implement `encoding.BinaryMarshaler`/`Unmarshaler`
  (+ `AppendBinary`, and binarystruct's `WriteBinary`/`ReadBinary`), and the
  binarystruct runtime auto-detects and fast-paths to them.
- **Also does:** export a struct's binary layout as JSON (`-json`), for schemas or
  non-Go clients; emits context-aware `...WithMarshaler` methods automatically when
  a struct uses text encodings / custom codecs / custom `valueof`.
- **Not for:** defining the tag syntax or doing runtime (un)marshalling — that's the
  [binarystruct library](https://github.com/mixcode/binarystruct) itself (this tool
  *compiles* its tags; the generated code calls into it). Not a general Go codegen
  tool. Not for structs using the runtime-only shapes codegen excludes (below) —
  those fail loud; use the runtime interpreter for them.

## 2. Invocation contract

Installed as a **separate nested Go module** (its own `go.mod`; Model A — files ship
with the module):

```
go install github.com/mixcode/binarystruct/binarystruct-codegen@latest
# pin a release with the PATH-PREFIXED nested-module tag (not a bare @vX.Y.Z):
go install github.com/mixcode/binarystruct/binarystruct-codegen@binarystruct-codegen/v0.3.5
```

The binary lands in `$GOBIN` (or `$(go env GOPATH)/bin`); ensure it's on `PATH`.

```
binarystruct-codegen -type TypeName[,TypeName2,...] [flags] [directory]
binarystruct-codegen -h          # usage banner (flag.Usage)
```

`[directory]` is the Go package dir containing the structs (default `.`).

| Flag | Meaning (default) | Effect |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| `-type` | comma-separated struct names (**required**) | — |
| `-endian big\|little` | **fallback** byte order baked into the no-arg methods; **optional** when the struct declares its own via a blank `_ struct{}` `binary:"endian=…"` field (which wins). Not needed with `-json`. | — |
| `-output` | output filename (default `<first_type>_binary.go`, or `<first_type>.json` with `-json`) | **writes** |
| `-json` | emit JSON layout metadata instead of Go code | **writes** |
| `-tests` | include `*_test.go` when parsing the package | — |
| `-no-validate` | strip ALL decode-time validation from generated readers (`const`/`range`/`match` + custom-`valueof` recompute); default off (validates everything, matching the runtime) | — |
| `-unsafe-bulk` | raw-memory bulk path (via `unsafe`) for fixed-width scalar arrays/slices whose Go element width matches the wire width; byte-identical, SIMD-accelerated under `-tags experiment_simd`; default off | — |

## 3. Auth / environment prerequisites (headless use)

**None.** No network, no tokens, no config. It reads local Go source and writes one
file. Non-interactive — it never prompts, so it is safe under `go generate` / CI.

## 4. Output, errors, and side effects

- **Output:** on success prints `Generated binarystruct methods for <types> -> <file>`
  (or the JSON variant) to **stdout**. There is no `--json`-status / quiet mode — the
  signal is the exit code + the written file.
- **Exit codes:** `0` on success; `1` on any failure (missing `-type` prints the usage
  banner then exits 1; generation/IO errors print `binarystruct-codegen: <error>` to
  **stderr** then exit 1). Errors are descriptive — they name the type/field and the
  fix (e.g. a no-byte-order error names an ordered parent that references the type).
- **Side effects (every run mutates the filesystem):** it **creates or overwrites**
  `<first_type>_binary.go` (or `.json`) in the target directory — a
  `// Code generated … DO NOT EDIT.` file. There is **no dry-run**; output is
  deterministic, so re-running is safe but will overwrite hand edits. Commit the
  generated file.

## 5. Traps (where an agent will otherwise guess wrong)

- **Pin with the path-prefixed tag.** `@binarystruct-codegen/vX.Y.Z`, *not* a bare
  `@vX.Y.Z` — the parent `binarystruct` module's tag does not select this tool.
- **Generated code may need a `*Marshaler`.** A struct using `encoding=`, `codec=`, or
  a custom `valueof=NAME(...)` evaluator **must** be driven via
  `WriteBinaryWithMarshaler` / `ReadBinaryWithMarshaler` with a configured Marshaler
  (register codecs/encodings/evaluators on it). The no-arg `MarshalBinary` /
  `UnmarshalBinary` pass a `nil` Marshaler and return a clear error for those fields.
- **`-endian` is the fallback, not required.** If the struct declares its order via
  the `_ struct{}` sentinel, omit `-endian`. Generation fails loud only when *neither*
  the struct nor the flag gives an order. Because each `-type` is processed in
  isolation, a nested type that inherits its order from a parent at runtime needs
  `-endian` (or its own declaration) when generated on its own — the error names the
  ordered parent.
- **Some shapes are intentionally runtime-only (fail loud → use the interpreter):**
  multidimensional arrays over a non-scalar leaf, a custom `valueof` over a
  nested-struct arg, struct-level `endian=inverse` or order/encoding inheritance via
  embedding, and a self-referential `valueof=bytelen(F)` cycle. This is by design; the
  binarystruct runtime handles all of them.

## 6. Recipe (the common real-world invocation)

Wire generation into the package with a directive, then `go generate`:

```go
//go:generate binarystruct-codegen -type Packet,Header -endian big
type Packet struct {
	_     struct{} `binary:"endian=big"` // declares order; -endian then optional
	Magic uint32   `binary:"uint32,const=0x504B0304"`
	Body  []byte   `binary:"[16]byte"`
}
```

```
go generate ./...   # produces packet_binary.go; commit it
```

Use `-endian big|little` only as a fallback for structs that don't declare their own
order; prefer declaring it on the struct.

## 7. Contributor note

Modifying this generator? See the repo-root [AGENTS.txt](../AGENTS.txt). The single
most important invariant: a tag feature is implemented in **three parallel execution
paths that must stay byte-identical** — the safe reflection path
(`marshal.go`/`unmarshal.go`), the unsafe path (`unsafe_io.go`), and this generator
(`generator.go`). A change to one is a silent bug unless mirrored in all three; add
tests in every mode.
